stories.title: HMRC Accounts Show Record £966 Billion in U.K. Taxes Collected
stories.summary: HMRC's annual accounts reveal a record £966 billion in UK taxes collected, marking the highest total in the organisation's history.

stories.title: HMRC Sends Letters to 580,000 Ahead of New August 2026 Deadline
stories.summary: HMRC is contacting over 580,000 workers who should have enrolled in Making Tax Digital (MTD) but have not yet done so, ahead of the August 2026 submission deadline.

stories.title: 1 in 2 Small Businesses Unprepared for New Digital Tax Deadline
stories.summary: A Lloyds Banking Group survey finds that around half of small businesses affected by HMRC's Making Tax Digital requirements have still not completed sign-up, with an estimated 475,000 firms yet to comply.

stories.title: Home Office to Spend £250m on Protecting Jewish Communities
stories.summary: The government has announced more than £250m to increase policing in Jewish communities in England and Wales following a spate of violent antisemitic attacks, including deployment of an extra 500 officers.

stories.title: Extra £251m Funding Boost to Protect Jewish Communities Across England
stories.summary: Police forces across England are to receive a £251m funding boost to help protect Jewish communities, announced by the Home Office following a rise in antisemitic incidents.

stories.title: Protests Held Over Home Office Asylum Plan for MoD Base Near Bicester
stories.summary: Two groups of protesters have gathered to oppose Home Office plans to house 1,250 single adult male asylum seekers at a former Ministry of Defence site near Bicester, Oxfordshire.

stories.title: India-UK Free Trade Agreement Set to Take Effect on 15 July 2026
stories.summary: The landmark India-UK Free Trade Agreement signed under Prime Minister Starmer is scheduled to enter into force on 15 July 2026, representing a major milestone in post-Brexit trade policy.

stories.title: UK High Commissioner Meets Pakistan Foreign Minister to Discuss Bilateral Relations
stories.summary: British High Commissioner Jane Marriott met Pakistan's Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar to discuss UK-Pakistan relations and the evolving regional landscape.

stories.title: DWP PIP Reforms: Thousands of UK Seniors Face Payment Changes
stories.summary: Labour's latest changes to Personal Independence Payment (PIP) are set to affect thousands of UK seniors, with new eligibility criteria and adjusted payment rates.

stories.title: DWP Weekly News Round-Up: Benefits System Consultation Open Until 18 August
stories.summary: A DWP consultation on the UK welfare benefits system is open on GOV.UK until 11:59pm on 18 August 2026, as reported in the community weekly news round-up.

stories.title: DEFRA Publishes 30by30 Land Delivery Plan for England
stories.summary: DEFRA has published its official delivery plan setting out how the government will achieve the UK's commitment to protect 30% of land in England by 2030, including criteria for land assessment.

stories.title: Heatwave Reducing Milk Production, Says Warwickshire Farmer
stories.summary: A Warwickshire dairy farmer reports that this summer's extreme heat has significantly reduced milk production, adding to existing financial pressures on the UK farming industry.

stories.title: UK Leads Europe with £3.16m Contracts for Low-Cost Air Defence Systems
stories.summary: The Ministry of Defence has awarded £3.16 million to three suppliers to develop low-cost interceptors designed to shoot down drones, making the UK the first European partner to act under a joint defence programme.

stories.title: Protests Held at Former MoD Base Earmarked for Asylum Seekers
stories.summary: Two groups of protesters assembled to oppose Home Office plans to house 1,250 single adult male asylum seekers at the former MoD site near Bicester, Oxfordshire.

stories.title: India-UK Free Trade Agreement Enters Into Force on 15 July 2026
stories.summary: The India-UK FTA, signed under Prime Minister Starmer, is set to take effect on 15 July 2026, opening significant new trade opportunities for British businesses.

stories.title: British Business Bank Allocates £17.5m to SWIG Finance for South West SMEs
stories.summary: SWIG Finance has been allocated £17.5m by the British Business Bank to support smaller businesses across the South West of England, boosting access to finance for local SMEs.

stories.title: Analysis: Making Sense of the UK's AI Security Institute
stories.summary: The Ada Lovelace Institute has published a detailed explainer on the UK's AI Security Institute (AISI) and how it fits into the broader UK AI governance landscape, as DSIT continues shaping the country's approach to AI regulation.

stories.title: Just One in Five GPs Now Works Full-Time
stories.summary: Official NHS data reveals that only one in five GPs is now working full-time, as younger doctors increasingly shun the traditional full-time working week, raising concerns about GP capacity.

stories.title: NHS Modernisation Bill Represents Most Consequential Health Reshape in a Decade
stories.summary: The government's recently announced NHS Modernisation Bill is described as one of the most significant attempts to reshape the health service in over a decade, aiming to reform structures and improve patient outcomes.

stories.title: Some NHS Services May Be Affected by Industrial Action
stories.summary: NHS services may face disruption due to ongoing industrial action; patients are advised to continue attending appointments unless contacted directly by their provider.

stories.title: Ofcom Launches Fresh Investigation into Royal Mail Over Missed Delivery Targets
stories.summary: The UK postal regulator Ofcom has opened a new investigation into Royal Mail after the company again failed to meet its delivery targets, with MPs also raising concerns about prioritisation of parcels over letters.

stories.title: 52 Post Office Branches Closing This Summer Due to Morrisons Exit
stories.summary: A decision by Morrisons supermarket to end its Post Office partnership will result in 52 Post Office branches closing this summer, affecting communities across the UK.
